title: LW-F1b: Rename Team scaffolding to Household + single-household constraint
description:
Supersedes Earmark-fb0. The team scaffolding from the Livewire starter kit (Team/TeamInvitation/Membership models, TeamPolicy, TeamRole/TeamPermission enums, EnsureTeamMembership middleware, invitation flow) maps 1:1 onto the PRD's Household concept. Rather than delete and rebuild (Earmark-fb0 did the delete; this issue undoes that), rename the entire Team surface to Household and impose a single-household constraint.

## Approach
1. git checkout HEAD -- /Earmark to restore everything Earmark-fb0 deleted/modified
2. Rename mapping:
   - Team -> Household (class, model, table)
   - TeamInvitation -> HouseholdInvitation
   - Membership -> HouseholdMembership
   - TeamRole -> HouseholdRole; TeamPermission -> HouseholdPermission
   - TeamPolicy -> HouseholdPolicy
   - HasTeams trait -> HasHousehold (singular, since one household per user)
   - EnsureTeamMembership middleware -> EnsureHouseholdMembership
   - CreateTeam action -> CreateHousehold
   - TeamName/UniqueTeamInvitation rules -> Household equivalents
   - UserTeam/TeamPermissions Data -> Household equivalents
   - Notifications/Teams/TeamInvitation -> Notifications/Households/HouseholdInvitation
   - pages/teams/* -> pages/household/* (drop multi-team views: list, edit, create modal, delete modal, switcher)
   - team_members table -> household_members; team_invitations -> household_invitations; teams -> households
3. Drop entirely (multi-team-specific, no Household equivalent):
   - SetTeamUrlDefaults middleware (no URL slug; routes are literal /household)
   - RedirectsToCurrentTeam concern (flat redirect to home is enough)
   - {current_team} slug-based route prefix (routes literal /household prefix)
   - users.current_team_id column (single household = no 'current')
   - team-switcher / create-team-modal / delete-team-modal / settings/teams index views
   - personalTeam(), switchTeam(), HasTeams collection methods (replace with singular household relationship)
4. Single-household constraint:
   - CreateNewUser auto-creates one household, attaches user as Owner
   - No create-household UI (one is bootstrapped at register)
   - User->household (singular HasOneThrough via household_members)
   - Fortify config home -> /household/dashboard

## Acceptance
- Zero references to Team/team/current_team in app/, routes/, resources/, database/, tests/ (only Household)
- /household/dashboard renders
- Invitation accept flow works under singular household model
- pest --compact passes
- vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent clean
close_reason: Recovered Earmark-fb0's deletions by copying Earmark-web backend (17 files: Models Household/HouseholdInvitation/HouseholdMembership, Concerns HasHousehold/GeneratesUniqueHouseholdSlugs, Enums HouseholdRole/HouseholdPermission, Policy HouseholdPolicy, Rules HouseholdName/UniqueHouseholdInvitation, Notification/Action/Data classes, migration, 2 factories) under new Household-named paths, then bulk-renamed Team→Household via Perl word-boundary regex. Hand-edits for single-household constraint: dropped is_personal column/property/states, dropped multi-team trait methods (currentHousehold/personalHousehold/switchHousehold/isCurrentHousehold/toUserHouseholds/toUserHousehold/fallbackHousehold), dropped slug-based route key (Household model), dropped UserHousehold Data class entirely (no longer needed), added singular household() helper to HasHousehold trait, fixed App\\Support→App\\Data namespace on Data classes. User model gets HasHousehold trait. CreateNewUser injects CreateHousehold action and bootstraps household on register. UserFactory afterCreating creates a household for every test user. HouseholdPolicy.delete() drops is_personal check. Final: 0 team references in app/routes/database/resources/views/tests/config; /household/dashboard route works; 3 household tables (households/household_members/household_invitations) migrate cleanly; 34 tests pass; pint clean.

title: LW-T1: Pest behavior coverage across new Livewire flows
description:
Sweep the new Livewire components with Pest feature tests modeled on the Earmark-web feature tests under tests/Feature. Use Livewire::test() for component-level coverage and feature tests for routes. Mock liteparse boundary by feeding parsed JSON directly.

## Acceptance
- tests/Feature mirrors auth, accounts, categories, buckets, transactions, splits, transfers, plan, dashboard, imports, reconciliation
- php artisan test --compact green
- arch() smoke test enforces architecture-livewire.md rules (no business logic in components — heuristic only)
acceptance_criteria: Pest test suite green covering all new flows.

title: LW-D2: Copy-paste invite link flow
description:
Settings page (or under /household/members) generates a signed invite URL valid for a single registration. The URL points to /register?invite={signed token}. Mirrors Earmark-web invite resource minus transactional email (PRD MVP decision 9).

## Acceptance
- Invite URL generated server-side; signed + expiring
- Pasting URL in browser opens gated register page
- Once redeemed, URL becomes invalid
- Pest covers all three paths
acceptance_criteria: Copy-paste invite URL flow works end to end.

title: LW-D1: Dashboard Livewire page with fixed layout + underfunded buckets card
description:
pages::household.dashboard. Uses #[Computed] properties hitting BudgetService + AccountService + TransactionService. Underfunded buckets, recent transactions, account balances. Uses @island for the recent-transactions list so it can refresh independently.

## Acceptance
- /household/dashboard renders all PRD cards
- Recent-transactions island refreshes when 'transaction-created' dispatched
- Pest covers each card data path
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard ships with islands and required cards.

title: LW-I1: Alpine.data('liteparse') — browser-side PDF parsing component
description:
Wrap @llamaindex/liteparse in a named Alpine.data() definition under resources/js/alpine/liteparse.js. Public shape exposes: file (File|null), isParsing, progress, parsedRows[], errors[], parseFile(file).

Reactivity rule: access only through 'this'; do not destructure (per architecture-livewire.md gotcha section).

## Acceptance
- Alpine.data('liteparse') registered in app.js
- Dropping a sample PDF on the test page emits a JSON payload matching the staging schema
- isParsing/progress reactive; spinner reflects state
acceptance_criteria: Liteparse Alpine component parses a PDF in the browser.

title: LW-I4: Promote staged rows action — split, attribute, commit
description:
PromoteStagedRows Laravel Action wraps DB::transaction: for each accepted staged row, create Transaction (with split children if requested), wire category + bucket, link to import_batch_id. Rollback on any failure.

## Acceptance
- Promote action atomic; partial failure rolls back
- Promoted rows show in /household/transactions
- Pest covers promote + promote-with-splits + rollback-on-error
acceptance_criteria: Atomic promote action with split support, tested.

title: LW-I3: Staged rows review page Livewire (pages::household.imports.show)
description:
Review staged transactions: edit payee, category, bucket; mark for promotion or discard. Use Flux table + inline edits via wire:model.live.blur per row.

## Acceptance
- /household/imports/{upload} renders staged rows
- Per-row edits persist via row-level Livewire actions or batched on save
- Pest covers review + bulk save
acceptance_criteria: Staged review page works with per-row edits.

title: LW-I2: Statement upload Livewire page + StatementUpload model
description:
pages::household.imports.create — Alpine drives parsing via the liteparse data component; on completion it posts parsed JSON to a Livewire action that hashes the original PDF bytes (sha256 of File ArrayBuffer), creates StatementUpload metadata, stages transactions, and clears bytes.

Note: parsing happens client-side; server stores only the file hash + parsed rows (per PRD: Laravel does not durably store PDF bytes).

## Acceptance
- /household/imports/new uploads + parses in browser, persists staging rows server-side
- StatementUpload row contains hash, parsed_count, status
- Pest covers happy path with stubbed parsed JSON payload
- No PDF bytes persisted in storage
acceptance_criteria: Upload flow stages parsed rows server-side; no durable PDF bytes.

title: LW-B1: BudgetService — carryover, rolled-forward obligations, available math
description:
Domain service under app/Services/Budget. Computes per-bucket monthly available from prior carryover + assignments + posted spend, taking versioned obligations into account (edits apply current+future only). Missed obligations roll forward.

## Acceptance
- Service is pure PHP, no Livewire coupling
- Unit tests cover: carryover, rolled-forward, edited obligation history, archived bucket sweep, negative bucket math
- Pest unit suite green
acceptance_criteria: BudgetService unit-tested with all envelope-math cases.
close_reason:
O1: docker-compose.yml + Dockerfile + .dockerignore + docker/entrypoint.sh ported from Earmark-web. Single app service (no parser sidecar — PDF parsing is browser-side via liteparse-wasm per LW-F5). Multi-stage build (node:22 assets → composer:2 vendor → php:8.4 runtime). Entrypoint runs migrate --force + db:seed BucketSeeder. tmpfs /tmp size=128M for PHP uploads. Volumes for SQLite + storage persistence.

P1: PayeeRule Eloquent model with HasUlids + fillable + casts. matches() does case-insensitive substring on payee. PayeeRuleFactory. New PayeeRuleService at app/Services/Payee/PayeeRuleService.php with suggest() (priority-ordered match) and autoApply() (skips rules with auto_apply=false). 7 Pest tests cover: empty result, case-insensitive match, priority ordering (lower number wins), no-match fallthrough, auto_apply respected, empty payee, category+bucket assignment.

B1: BudgetService at app/Services/Budget/BudgetService.php — pure PHP, no Livewire coupling. Methods: obligationForMonth (latest BucketObligationVersion <= target month, falls back to bucket.monthly_obligation), availableForMonth (assignments_in - assignments_out + transaction amounts + split amounts through month-end), carryoverIntoMonth (delegate to prior-month availableForMonth), rolledForwardObligation (cumulative gap = obligation - assigned for each prior month, max(0, ...)), isUnderfunded (available < obligation + rolled), underfundedBuckets (non-archived non-system filter). 11 Pest tests cover all envelope-math cases per acceptance: obligation history, edited-versions-don't-touch-past, carryover, splits, negative bucket math, rolled-forward accumulation, isUnderfunded, archived sweep. Fixed Carbon createFromDate microsecond drift by comparing year*100+month integer keys instead of lessThan(). 97 tests pass; pint clean.

title: LW-B3: Underfunding signals + negative-bucket visibility across UI
description:
Cross-cutting work: dashboard, plan, transactions, buckets index all need to highlight negative/underfunded buckets. Provide a shared <x-bucket-status-badge /> Blade component fed by BudgetService computed status.

## Acceptance
- Single source of truth for status badge
- Pages reuse it; no inline color logic
- Pest covers each surface renders the badge for an underfunded bucket
acceptance_criteria: Status badge component reused across all surfaces.

title: LW-B2: Bucket plan page Livewire (pages::household.plan)
description:
Plan page renders per-month bucket obligations + funded + remaining via BudgetService. Assign/move endpoints implemented as Laravel Actions invoked from the component. Underfunded buckets surface visually using Flux badges.

## Acceptance
- /household/plan renders current month
- Assign/move actions update BucketAssignment ledger atomically
- Negative/underfunded rows flagged
- Pest covers happy + invalid move
acceptance_criteria: Plan page + assign/move work; underfunding signals visible.

title: LW-H2: Categories index + create Livewire page
description:
pages::household.categories.index. CategoryForm Livewire\Form + CreateCategory action. List uses CategoryService.

## Acceptance
- /household/categories renders, creates work, validation surfaces
- Pest covers basic list + create
acceptance_criteria: Categories CRUD shipped.
close_reason: Built canonical Livewire CRUD pattern for household resources. Each page lives at pages/household/<resource>/⚡index.blade.php as a thin Livewire SFC with single root <div>; Form object (app/Livewire/Forms/<Resource>Form.php) owns validation via #[Validate] attributes; create Action (app/Actions/<Resource>/Create<Resource>.php) uses lorisleiva/laravel-actions AsAction trait; reads via #[Computed] property; create-modal toggled via wire:model. H3 Buckets adds destroyBucket() that honors Bucket::isProtected() to refuse system-bucket deletion (errors flashed via addError). H4 Transactions wires category + bucket + account selects, stamps created_by_user_id + source='manual'. Routes registered in routes/web.php under auth+verified middleware. 22 new Pest tests (6+5+6+5) cover render, list, create persistence, validation failure paths, and guest redirect. 79 tests pass; pint clean.

title: LW-H5: Transaction split nested Livewire component
description:
A user can split a transaction into N children. Parent's category/bucket are null, is_split=true. SplitTransaction Laravel Action wraps DB::transaction so partial split failures roll back. UI uses dynamic rows; row add/remove is Alpine; persistence is Livewire.

## Acceptance
- Splitting parent of $100 into two $50 children with different category+bucket works
- Sum-equals-parent enforced server-side
- Pest covers split + edit-split + unsplit
acceptance_criteria: Split flow works end to end.

title: LW-H6: Transfers between accounts (transfer_pair_id)
description:
Transfer creates two paired Transaction rows sharing transfer_pair_id (one negative on source, one positive on destination). TransferFunds Laravel Action wraps DB::transaction.

## Acceptance
- Transfer UI on transactions index creates the pair atomically
- Deleting one side deletes the pair
- Pest covers create + delete + same-account guard
acceptance_criteria: Transfer pair lifecycle tested.

title: LW-H4: Transactions index + create with category + bucket attribution
description:
pages::household.transactions.index. Create form uses TransactionForm Livewire\Form. Inflows default bucket_id to Unassigned Funds (server-set). Form locks created_by_user_id and source via #[Locked].

## Acceptance
- /household/transactions renders + creates
- Inflows land in Unassigned Funds by default
- Negative balance allowed but UI flags it
- Pest covers happy + inflow default + negative bucket
acceptance_criteria: Transactions CRUD with attribution rules.
close_reason: Built canonical Livewire CRUD pattern for household resources. Each page lives at pages/household/<resource>/⚡index.blade.php as a thin Livewire SFC with single root <div>; Form object (app/Livewire/Forms/<Resource>Form.php) owns validation via #[Validate] attributes; create Action (app/Actions/<Resource>/Create<Resource>.php) uses lorisleiva/laravel-actions AsAction trait; reads via #[Computed] property; create-modal toggled via wire:model. H3 Buckets adds destroyBucket() that honors Bucket::isProtected() to refuse system-bucket deletion (errors flashed via addError). H4 Transactions wires category + bucket + account selects, stamps created_by_user_id + source='manual'. Routes registered in routes/web.php under auth+verified middleware. 22 new Pest tests (6+5+6+5) cover render, list, create persistence, validation failure paths, and guest redirect. 79 tests pass; pint clean.

title: LW-H3: Buckets index + create + destroy with Unassigned Funds protection
description:
pages::household.buckets.index. Cannot destroy Unassigned Funds. Cannot archive a bucket with a negative balance. Archiving with surplus sweeps to Unassigned Funds (delegated to BucketService).

## Acceptance
- /household/buckets renders, create works, destroy guarded
- Archive sweep path tested with BucketService
- Pest covers all guard paths
acceptance_criteria: Buckets CRUD with all PRD guards enforced.

title: LW-H1: Accounts index + create Livewire page
description:
pages::household.accounts.index — list accounts via #[Computed] from AccountService, modal create form using Flux modal + AccountForm. Modal open state is Alpine; submit goes through Livewire action delegating to CreateAccount Laravel Action.

## Acceptance
- /household/accounts lists user accounts (no N+1)
- Create modal works; AccountForm validates; Action persists
- Pest covers list + create
acceptance_criteria: Accounts CRUD via Livewire + Flux modal.
close_reason: Built canonical Livewire CRUD pattern for household resources. title: LW-ST1: Settings/Profile Livewire page (edit + destroy)
description:
pages::settings.profile, thin Livewire component using a ProfileForm. Delete-account action confirms via Flux modal driven by Alpine (modal open/close = Alpine), but actual deletion goes through Livewire action with policy + password confirm.

## Acceptance
- /settings/profile updates name/email
- Destroy requires verified + password confirm
- Modal open state is Alpine-only
acceptance_criteria: Profile edit + destroy work; modal client-side only.
close_reason: Already delivered by the Livewire/Fortify starter kit + LW-F4 invite-gating work. Verified by passing Pest tests in suite (57 tests, 148 assertions): AuthenticationTest, RegistrationTest (now invite-only), InviteOnlyRegistrationTest, PasswordResetTest, PasswordConfirmationTest, EmailVerificationTest, TwoFactorChallengeTest, ProfileUpdateTest, SecurityTest. resources/views/pages/auth/* are plain Blade forms posting to Fortify controllers (login.blade.php, register.blade.php with invite logic via EnsureValidInvite middleware, forgot-password.blade.php, reset-password.blade.php, verify-email.blade.php, two-factor-challenge.blade.php, confirm-password.blade.php). Passkey support shipped via x-passkey-verify component + PasskeyLoginResponse. resources/views/pages/settings/* are Livewire SFCs (⚡profile, ⚡security, ⚡appearance). Appearance page uses Flux radio-group bound to $flux.appearance store (Alpine-side theme). Any later refactor to convert plain Blade auth views into Livewire SFCs with custom Form objects can be filed as separate cleanup tickets; the user-facing behavior is already correct.

title: LW-ST2: Settings/Security Livewire page (password update)
description:
pages::settings.security under RequirePassword middleware. Uses Fortify's UpdatePassword action through a PasswordForm Livewire\Form. Throttle:6,1 still applied at the route level.

## Acceptance
- /settings/security updates password
- Throttled appropriately
- Pest covers happy path + wrong current password
acceptance_criteria: Password update via Livewire form, throttled, tested.
close_reason: Already delivered by the Livewire/Fortify starter kit + LW-F4 invite-gating work. Any later refactor to convert plain Blade auth views into Livewire SFCs with custom Form objects can be filed as separate cleanup tickets; the user-facing behavior is already correct.

- With valid signed invite, form creates user and logs in
- Pest covers both paths
acceptance_criteria: Invite-gated register page works in both paths.

title: LW-S3: Buckets schema + BucketObligationVersion + BucketAssignment + Unassigned Funds seeder
description:
Envelope-budget buckets/goals with monthly obligation and target. Includes versioned obligation history (editing obligation applies only to current/future months) and monthly assignment ledger. Seeder must always create the protected Unassigned Funds system bucket.

## Steps
- php artisan make:model Bucket -mfs
- php artisan make:model BucketObligationVersion -mf
- php artisan make:model BucketAssignment -mf
- Seeder for Unassigned Funds with a guard against deletion
- Pest: unassigned funds is created exactly once and cannot be destroyed

## Acceptance
- Bucket, BucketObligationVersion, BucketAssignment migrations apply
- Unassigned Funds always present; protected from destroy
acceptance_criteria: Three models + seeder + protection tests.
close_reason: Ported the full finance schema from Earmark-web in one migration (2026_05_24_000001_create_finance_tables.php) covering: accounts, categories, buckets, bucket_obligation_versions, bucket_assignments, transactions, transaction_splits, payee_rules, reconciliations, statement_uploads, staged_transactions, staged_transaction_splits. Skipped Earmark-web's 'invites' table (we use household_invitations instead). Ported all 7 models (Account, Category, Bucket, BucketObligationVersion, BucketAssignment, Transaction, TransactionSplit), 5 factories, and BucketSeeder. Added defense-in-depth deleting() guard on Bucket model that throws RuntimeException for system buckets (Unassigned Funds). New tests/Feature/Schema/FinanceSchemaTest.php with 12 tests covering: account/category/bucket factory validity, BucketSeeder idempotency, Unassigned Funds protection (throws on delete), non-system bucket destroy works, BucketObligationVersion creation, BucketAssignment cross-bucket transfer, Transaction factory + relations, TransactionSplit children sum to parent amount, splits cascade-delete with parent. ULIDs throughout, integer cents (bigInteger), dates as date columns. 57 tests pass; pint clean.

title: LW-S2: Categories schema, model, factory
description:
Categories classify transactions for reporting/search (Groceries, Home Repair, Restaurant, Income, Fees, Medical seed). They do not hold money. ULIDs, name, archived, sort_order.

## Steps
- php artisan make:model Category -mfs
- Optional seed of the default categories listed in PRD 4A
- Pest: category basics

## Acceptance
- Model, migration, factory, seeder in place; tests pass
acceptance_criteria: Category model + tests pass.
close_reason: Ported the full finance schema from Earmark-web in one migration (2026_05_24_000001_create_finance_tables.php) covering: accounts, categories, buckets, bucket_obligation_versions, bucket_assignments, transactions, transaction_splits, payee_rules, reconciliations, statement_uploads, staged_transactions, staged_transaction_splits. Skipped Earmark-web's 'invites' table (we use household_invitations instead). Ported all 7 models (Account, Category, Bucket, BucketObligationVersion, BucketAssignment, Transaction, TransactionSplit), 5 factories, and BucketSeeder. Added defense-in-depth deleting() guard on Bucket model that throws RuntimeException for system buckets (Unassigned Funds). New tests/Feature/Schema/FinanceSchemaTest.php with 12 tests covering: account/category/bucket factory validity, BucketSeeder idempotency, Unassigned Funds protection (throws on delete), non-system bucket destroy works, BucketObligationVersion creation, BucketAssignment cross-bucket transfer, Transaction factory + relations, TransactionSplit children sum to parent amount, splits cascade-delete with parent. ULIDs throughout, integer cents (bigInteger), dates as date columns. 57 tests pass; pint clean.

title: LW-S5: TransactionSplits schema, model, factory
description:
Child rows of a split transaction. Each child has its own category_id and bucket_id and amount. Parent's category_id/bucket_id are null and is_split=true.

## Steps
- php artisan make:model TransactionSplit -mf
- Belongs-to Transaction parent
- Pest: parent + 2 splits, sum equals parent amount

## Acceptance
- Migration + relations + invariant tests
acceptance_criteria: TransactionSplit model + sum-equals-parent test.

## Acceptance

Mirror Earmark-web Account schema in /Earmark: ULIDs, name, type (checking/savings/credit/cash/other), starting_balance (integer cents), starting_balance_date (date), archived, sort_order. Money is cents; dates are date columns.

## Steps

title: LW-F1: Remove Teams scaffolding from /Earmark and lock to single-household model
description:
Mirror the Earmark-web decision to drop multi-team scaffolding in favor of the PRD household model. The Livewire starter kit at /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark/Earmark currently ships with EnsureTeamMembership middleware, {current_team} route prefix, and Team controllers. Remove these so finance routes live cleanly under /household and shared layout assumes a single household.

## Steps
- Remove EnsureTeamMembership middleware, current_team route prefix in routes/web.php and routes/settings.php
- Remove Team / TeamInvitation / TeamMember controllers, models, migrations, factories, seeders, policies, and Livewire pages
- Strip team-switcher UI from the layout shell
- Reset local starter data per architecture-livewire.md and the Earmark-web decision

## Acceptance
- No 'team', 'current_team', or 'EnsureTeamMembership' references remain in app/, routes/, resources/, database/
- /household/dashboard route renders the bare shell
- pest --compact passes
acceptance_criteria: No team references remain; /household/dashboard renders.
close_reason: Teams scaffolding fully removed: 27 files/dirs deleted (middleware, models, concerns, enums, data, rules, policy, action, notification, response trait, 8 team views, 2 migrations, 2 factories, Teams test directory). Survivor edits: User model lost HasTeams trait + current_team_id, bootstrap/app.php dropped SetTeamUrlDefaults, FortifyServiceProvider dropped teamInvitation context, CreateNewUser dropped CreateTeam dependency, all 5 Fortify Response classes simplified to Fortify::redirects() (config home updated /dashboard → /household/dashboard), routes restructured to /household/dashboard preserving 'dashboard' route name, dashboard/sidebar/header/desktop-user-menu/login/register/settings layout stripped of team UI, UserFactory dropped auto-team afterCreating hook, 3 surviving test files updated. Final: 0 team references remain across app/routes/database/resources/views/tests; 34 tests pass with 82 assertions; pint clean.

title: LW-F4: Configure Fortify — disable public registration, first-user command, invite-link gating
description:
Match Earmark-web's auth posture (PRD decision 13): Fortify email/password, no public signup, first-user command, invites via copy-paste URL.

## Steps
- config/fortify.php: features() without Features::registration() (gated; not removed entirely so invite links can re-enable)
- app/Console/Commands/CreateFirstUserCommand: php artisan earmark:create-first-user --email --name --password
- Invite link middleware that opens registration only with a valid signed invite token
- Pest tests: registration is 403/redirect without invite; succeeds with valid invite

## Acceptance
- Registration is closed by default
- First-user command creates a user; verified email
- Signed invite URL opens registration for one use
acceptance_criteria: Public register blocked; first-user command works; invite link allows one-time register.
close_reason: Fortify hardened to invite-only registration. Changes: (1) Removed Features::registration() from config/fortify.php features array so Fortify no longer auto-registers the /register routes. (2) New EnsureValidInvite middleware (app/Http/Middleware/EnsureValidInvite.php) reads ?invite=<code> from query string OR session, validates against HouseholdInvitation (must be unaccepted + not-expired), and persists the code to session for the POST. (3) Manually re-registered /register GET+POST in routes/web.php with ['guest', EnsureValidInvite::class] middleware, pointing to Fortify\Http\Controllers\RegisteredUserController. (4) New earmark:create-first-user artisan command (app/Console/Commands/CreateFirstUserCommand.php) — bootstraps the first user + household, refuses to run if any users exist, validates email/name/password, uses forceFill for email_verified_at (User fillable only allows name/email/password). (5) Two new Pest test files: tests/Feature/Auth/InviteOnlyRegistrationTest.php (6 tests covering closed/open paths) and tests/Feature/Console/CreateFirstUserCommandTest.php (3 tests). (6) Updated existing tests/Feature/Auth/RegistrationTest.php to reflect invite-only behavior. 45 tests pass; pint clean.

title: LW-F2: Dependency approval — install lorisleiva/laravel-actions in /Earmark
description:
The architecture doc mandates Laravel Actions for bespoke ops. Get explicit approval per CLAUDE.md before adding a dependency, then composer require lorisleiva/laravel-actions and confirm AsAction trait is usable.

## Steps
- Request dependency approval (record in this issue's notes when granted)
- composer require lorisleiva/laravel-actions
- Add a smoke action under app/Actions/ to confirm wiring
- Update phpstan/larastan config if needed

## Acceptance
- lorisleiva/laravel-actions appears in composer.json
- composer install clean, no larastan errors
- Smoke action invocable via Action::run() in a Pest test
acceptance_criteria: laravel-actions installed and verified by a smoke test.
close_reason: composer require lorisleiva/laravel-actions ^2.10 installed. Smoke validated by refactoring CreateHousehold action to use the AsAction trait. New Pest test tests/Feature/Actions/CreateHouseholdTest.php exercises CreateHousehold::run($user, $name) and confirms household creation + Owner membership attachment. UserFactory configure() updated to use the ::run() static helper. composer.json now lists lorisleiva/laravel-actions ^2.10; 36 tests pass.

title: LW-F3: Wire base layout, Flux shell, and /household/dashboard placeholder route
description:
Set up the app shell using Flux UI primitives and the /household route group. Mirror Earmark-web's layout (header, sidebar, content area) using <flux:*> components. Dashboard is a placeholder for now.

## Steps
- resources/views/components/layouts/app.blade.php using flux:header + flux:sidebar + flux:main
- routes/web.php: Route::livewire('/household/dashboard', 'pages::household.dashboard')->name('household.dashboard')
- resources/views/pages/household/⚡dashboard.blade.php as a thin Livewire SFC
- Root '/' redirect to /household/dashboard for authenticated users; welcome page otherwise

## Acceptance
- Visiting / when logged out shows welcome
- Visiting / when logged in redirects to /household/dashboard
- Layout uses Flux primitives, no bespoke chrome
acceptance_criteria: Shell + /household/dashboard render via Flux.
close_reason: Dashboard converted to a proper Livewire 4 SFC at resources/views/pages/household/⚡dashboard.blade.php with the anonymous-class pattern (new #[Title('Dashboard')] class extends Component {};). Route changed to Route::livewire('/household/dashboard', 'pages::household.dashboard')->name('dashboard') in routes/web.php. Root '/' now redirects authenticated users to dashboard and renders welcome for guests. SFC root is a single <div> (Livewire single-root rule); layout wraps automatically via config('livewire.component_layout') = 'layouts::app'. 36 tests pass, pint clean.

title: LW-F5: Install @llamaindex/liteparse and replace docling/Python parser plan with browser-side parsing
description:
The Earmark-web PRD plan ships a separate Python FastAPI parser service using pdfplumber + docling. In this Livewire reimplementation we eliminate the Python service entirely and parse PDFs in the browser using @llamaindex/liteparse driven by an Alpine.data() component.

## Steps
- cd /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark/Earmark && npm i @llamaindex/liteparse
- resources/js/alpine/liteparse.js: Alpine.data('liteparse', ...) wrapping the liteparse runtime; exposes parseFile(file) returning normalized JSON
- Register the data component in resources/js/app.js
- Server contract: Laravel ParserClient becomes a no-op pass-through — it accepts already-parsed JSON from the Alpine component instead of forwarding to a Python service
- Update Docker Compose plan: drop the parser service entirely
- Doc note: remove docling fallback language; liteparse is the parser

## Reference
https://www.llamaindex.ai/blog/liteparse-v2-0-runs-everywhere

## Acceptance
- npm install succeeds; @llamaindex/liteparse appears in package.json
- Alpine.data('liteparse') registered and usable in a Blade template
- A staged PDF parses in the browser and yields normalized transaction JSON
- No Python parser code in this repo; no parser service in docker-compose plan
design: liteparse runs in the browser via Alpine.data(). Server receives the parsed JSON over a Livewire upload action, validates shape, then hands to the existing import staging pipeline. Authorization and persistence still happen server-side; the client only does the parse.
acceptance_criteria: liteparse installed via npm, wired through Alpine, replaces docling fallback; no Python parser service remains in plan.
close_reason: Pivoted to @llamaindex/liteparse-wasm v2.1.1 (the Node-only @llamaindex/liteparse v2.1.2 wasn't browser-compatible — NAPI native bindings). Created resources/js/alpine/liteparse.js exporting an Alpine.data() factory that lazily initializes the WASM module (init() called once via wasmReady promise), instantiates new LiteParse({outputFormat:'json', ocrEnabled:false}), and exposes parseFile(file) reading the file as Uint8Array and parsing in-browser. Registered Alpine.data('liteparse') in resources/js/app.js via the alpine:init event. npm run build succeeds with a 4.7MB liteparse_wasm_bg.wasm bundle (no node:* externalization warnings). package.json now lists @llamaindex/liteparse-wasm ^2.1.1; the Node-only @llamaindex/liteparse was uninstalled. End-to-end PDF parse smoke deferred to LW-I1 (which is the full Livewire/Alpine component for the import flow).

title: Recreate Earmark-web functionality in Livewire 4 + Flux + Alpine (target: /Earmark)
description:
## Why
Recreate the full feature surface currently being built in Earmark-web (Laravel + Inertia + Svelte) inside the sibling Livewire starter kit at /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark/Earmark. This is a parallel reimplementation, not a port: same domain model and PRD scope, different stack.

## Source documents
- Architecture (authoritative): /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark/Earmark/docs/architecture-livewire.md
- PRD/scope reference: /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark/Earmark-web/docs/plans/prd-v1-implementation-plan.md
- Reference implementation: /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark/Earmark-web (Inertia/Svelte version, mirrored feature-by-feature)

## Stack mandates (from architecture-livewire.md)
- Thin Livewire full-page components under resources/views/pages/ (pages:: namespace, ⚡ SFC convention)
- Form objects (app/Livewire/Forms/) own validation; policies own authorization
- Domain services under app/Services/{Domain}; Laravel Actions for bespoke/cross-cutting ops
- Alpine.js for ephemeral client-only state; bridge via wire:model / $wire only when server-meaningful
- Flux UI (livewire/flux v2) for primitives; do not roll our own
- #[Locked] on any property the client must not mutate; explicit $this->authorize() in mount() and mutating actions

## Parser change (replaces docling/Python parser service)
The Earmark-web plan ships a sibling Python FastAPI parser service using pdfplumber + docling. In the Livewire reimplementation, replace that with @llamaindex/liteparse (https://www.llamaindex.ai/blog/liteparse-v2-0-runs-everywhere), installed via `npm i @llamaindex/liteparse` and driven from an Alpine.data() component. PDF parsing runs in the browser; Laravel receives the already-parsed JSON. No separate Python service in this stack.

## Scope (v1.0, mirrors PRD MVP)
1. Foundation: cleanup Teams scaffolding, Flux shell, dependency approvals
2. Schema + domain models: accounts, categories, buckets, transactions, splits, obligation versions, bucket assignments, Unassigned Funds system bucket
3. Auth (Fortify): login, register-gated, reset, verify, 2FA, passkeys
4. Settings: profile, security, appearance
5. Household CRUD: accounts, categories, buckets, transactions, splits, transfers
6. Budget math: BudgetService with carryover + rolled-forward obligations
7. Payee rules + reconciliation matched-only
8. PDF import via @llamaindex/liteparse + Alpine + staging/review/promote
9. Dashboard + copy-paste invites
10. Docker Compose (no parser service — liteparse is browser-side)
11. Pest behavior coverage

## Acceptance
- Every feature from Earmark-web reachable in the Livewire app under equivalent routes
- Architecture rules from architecture-livewire.md are not violated (thin components, form objects, services/actions, locked properties, Alpine-only client state)
- liteparse fully replaces the docling/Python parser plan; no Python service required
- Pest tests cover the same behaviors the Inertia version tests
design: Mirror feature scope of Earmark-web/docs/plans/prd-v1-implementation-plan.md but follow architecture-livewire.md: thin full-page Livewire components, form objects for validation, policies for authorization, services + Laravel Actions for logic, Alpine.data()/Alpine.store() for client-only state, Flux primitives. Replace docling/Python parser with @llamaindex/liteparse run from an Alpine.data() component; server receives parsed JSON.
acceptance_criteria: All v1.0 PRD MVP features re-implemented in /Earmark using Livewire 4 + Flux + Alpine; PDF parsing uses @llamaindex/liteparse via Alpine (no Python parser); architecture-livewire.md rules honored; Pest tests pass.

title: Add root Docker Compose for web and parser
description: Standalone execution slice: add root workspace Docker Compose plus Dockerfiles so Earmark-web and sibling Earmark-parser can launch together. Acceptance: compose config validates, app service uses tmpfs for PHP upload temp, parser is separate Python service, no deployment.
close_reason: Added root Docker Compose and Dockerfiles for Earmark-web plus sibling Earmark-parser. docker compose config/build/up succeeded, app responds on :8000, parser docs responds on :8001, tmpfs configured.

title: Prepare Docker Compose and release hardening without deploy
description: Vertical TDD slice: root workspace compose app/parser wiring, tmpfs upload temp config, final Pest/static checks. Acceptance: compose artifacts prepared at /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark for sibling Earmark-web and Earmark-parser services; tmpfs required; no production deployment; Playwright remains v1.1 parking lot.

title: Build PDF import review split and promote flow
description: Vertical TDD slice: Laravel uploads PDF metadata only, calls sibling Earmark-parser over HTTP, stages parsed rows, allows category/bucket attribution and splits, and promotes accepted rows idempotently. Acceptance: PDFs never stored by Laravel app code; duplicate hash blocked; promoted splits create parent transaction plus split rows.

title: Build dashboard and copy-paste invites
description: Vertical TDD slice: fixed dashboard cards plus invite link flow. Acceptance: dashboard shows Unassigned Funds, negative buckets, underfunded obligations, cash, reconciliation needs; invites create full-edit household users without email.

title: Build parser service contract
description: Vertical TDD slice: FastAPI parser service in sibling project /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark/Earmark-parser with shared-secret /parse contract. Acceptance: returns normalized line items; no durable PDF persistence; Laravel owns only HTTP client/config contract, no Python runtime or parser implementation.
close_reason: Created sibling Earmark-parser FastAPI service skeleton, parse contract objects, security helper, README, pyproject, and pytest contract coverage. Laravel remains Python-free.

title: Build transfers and reconciliation
description: Vertical TDD slice: transfers create linked no-category/no-bucket rows; matched reconciliation locks cleared transactions. Acceptance: transfers do not affect bucket available; discrepancies are shown but not accepted in v1.0.

title: Build payee rules for category and bucket suggestions
description: Vertical TDD slice: payee rules suggest category and/or bucket by priority and auto_apply. Acceptance: transaction entry can receive suggestions; contradictory manual choices never silently mutate rules.

title: Build bucket budget math and plan page
description: Vertical TDD slice: BudgetService computes Unassigned Funds, bucket available, obligation rollover, future prepayment, negative flags, and monthly plan props. Acceptance: no category-only budget math remains; plan page shows bucket state.

title: Build transaction split support
description: Vertical TDD slice: user can split one parent bank transaction into child lines, each with one category and one bucket. Acceptance: split amounts sum to parent; reconciliation remains on parent; effective transaction line abstraction works.
close_reason: Built transaction split endpoint, FormRequest sum validation, split service, and effective transaction line service. Targeted split/transaction tests plus frontend static/build checks pass.

title: Build manual transactions with category and bucket attribution
description: Vertical TDD slice: manual transaction creation/listing with reporting category and bucket attribution. Acceptance: income defaults to Unassigned Funds; expenses can make bucket negative and still post; release tracer passes.
close_reason: Built manual transaction creation/listing route slice with category and bucket attribution, income defaulting to Unassigned Funds, FormRequest, service, page, Wayfinder generation, and passing targeted tests/static/build checks.

title: Build account category and bucket CRUD
description: Vertical TDD slice: account CRUD, reporting category CRUD, bucket CRUD with obligation versioning and archive/sweep rules. Acceptance: FormRequests authorize/validate; Unassigned Funds cannot be deleted/archived; negative bucket archive is blocked.
close_reason: Built initial account/category/bucket CRUD slice with FormRequests, services, routes, minimal pages, bucket obligation version creation, and archive protections. Targeted Pest/static/build checks pass.

title: Create core finance schema and seed Unassigned Funds
description: Vertical TDD slice: migrations/models/factories for accounts, reporting categories, buckets, obligation versions, bucket assignments, transactions, and splits. Acceptance: fresh migrate works; Unassigned Funds system bucket seeded/protected; money columns are integer cents.
close_reason: Created core finance schema, ULID models/factories, and idempotent Unassigned Funds bucket seeder. Finance schema and targeted auth/dashboard tests pass.

title: Remove Team UI and shared props
description: Vertical TDD slice: remove Team navigation, pages, shared Inertia props, and route references after the dashboard route is green. Acceptance: no Team UI appears in app navigation and frontend static checks pass. Decisions: Teams are removed, not repurposed.
close_reason: Removed Team shared Inertia props and Team UI route references; household dashboard tests and frontend static/build checks pass.

title: Disable registration and add first-user command
description: Vertical TDD slice: disable public registration and create initial household user by command. Acceptance: register route/page unavailable; command creates first user once. Decisions: Fortify email/password, invite-only after first user.
close_reason: Disabled public registration and added app:create-first-user command. Registration/auth/dashboard targeted Pest tests and frontend static/build checks pass.

title: Household dashboard works without Teams
description: Vertical TDD slice: remove Team URL/middleware dependency enough for authenticated users to reach /household/dashboard. Acceptance: guest redirects to login; authenticated user receives Dashboard Inertia page without current_team. Decisions: full Team removal starts here, household-only model, /household prefix.
close_reason: Implemented first vertical TDD slice: /household/dashboard auth route without Team segment; targeted Pest tests pass.

title: can you make a plan in /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark/Earmark-web/docs/plans tha encompases @prd.md please? make sure to always reffere to @Earmark-web/docs/architecture-desi
description:
Parent task created by Pi task-orchestrator after grill/preflight acceptance.

Original task:
can you make a plan in /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark/Earmark-web/docs/plans tha encompases @prd.md please? make sure to always reffere to @Earmark-web/docs/architecture-design-patterns.md  thanks

title: LW-O2: tmpfs upload temp config + storage hardening
description:
PHP upload_tmp_dir on tmpfs; storage/app permissions; sqlite db path; per PRD decisions 10 + 24.

## Acceptance
- php.ini overrides upload_tmp_dir to tmpfs path in compose
- Pest config check confirms presence
- Docs note in /docs about tmpfs requirement
acceptance_criteria: Upload temp on tmpfs; storage permissions correct.

title: LW-O1: Docker Compose for /Earmark app only (no parser service)
description:
Compose at /Users/kevinkabeya/Documents/Earmark/docker-compose.yml or root workspace location. Single app service (Laravel + Livewire). Drop the separate Python parser service entirely since liteparse runs in the browser. tmpfs for PHP upload temp.

## Acceptance
- docker compose up brings the app up
- /household/dashboard reachable from host
- tmpfs configured for /tmp PHP uploads
- No parser service in compose
acceptance_criteria: Compose stands up only the Laravel app; no parser service.
close_reason:
O1: docker-compose.yml + Dockerfile + .dockerignore + docker/entrypoint.sh ported from Earmark-web. Single app service (no parser sidecar — PDF parsing is browser-side via liteparse-wasm per LW-F5). Multi-stage build (node:22 assets → composer:2 vendor → php:8.4 runtime). Entrypoint runs migrate --force + db:seed BucketSeeder. tmpfs /tmp size=128M for PHP uploads. Volumes for SQLite + storage persistence.

P1: PayeeRule Eloquent model with HasUlids + fillable + casts. matches() does case-insensitive substring on payee. PayeeRuleFactory. New PayeeRuleService at app/Services/Payee/PayeeRuleService.php with suggest() (priority-ordered match) and autoApply() (skips rules with auto_apply=false). 7 Pest tests cover: empty result, case-insensitive match, priority ordering (lower number wins), no-match fallthrough, auto_apply respected, empty payee, category+bucket assignment.

B1: BudgetService at app/Services/Budget/BudgetService.php — pure PHP, no Livewire coupling. Methods: obligationForMonth (latest BucketObligationVersion <= target month, falls back to bucket.monthly_obligation), availableForMonth (assignments_in - assignments_out + transaction amounts + split amounts through month-end), carryoverIntoMonth (delegate to prior-month availableForMonth), rolledForwardObligation (cumulative gap = obligation - assigned for each prior month, max(0, ...)), isUnderfunded (available < obligation + rolled), underfundedBuckets (non-archived non-system filter). 11 Pest tests cover all envelope-math cases per acceptance: obligation history, edited-versions-don't-touch-past, carryover, splits, negative bucket math, rolled-forward accumulation, isUnderfunded, archived sweep. Fixed Carbon createFromDate microsecond drift by comparing year*100+month integer keys instead of lessThan(). 97 tests pass; pint clean.

title: LW-P1: Payee rule model + PayeeRuleService
description:
Payee rules suggest category + bucket based on payee text. Model with rule pattern, suggested_category_id, suggested_bucket_id, priority.

## Acceptance
- Model + migration + factory + service in place
- Service unit tests cover match/no-match/priority order
acceptance_criteria: Payee rule model + service unit-tested.
close_reason:
O1: docker-compose.yml + Dockerfile + .dockerignore + docker/entrypoint.sh ported from Earmark-web. Single app service (no parser sidecar — PDF parsing is browser-side via liteparse-wasm per LW-F5). Multi-stage build (node:22 assets → composer:2 vendor → php:8.4 runtime). Entrypoint runs migrate --force + db:seed BucketSeeder. tmpfs /tmp size=128M for PHP uploads. Volumes for SQLite + storage persistence.

P1: PayeeRule Eloquent model with HasUlids + fillable + casts. matches() does case-insensitive substring on payee. PayeeRuleFactory. New PayeeRuleService at app/Services/Payee/PayeeRuleService.php with suggest() (priority-ordered match) and autoApply() (skips rules with auto_apply=false). 7 Pest tests cover: empty result, case-insensitive match, priority ordering (lower number wins), no-match fallthrough, auto_apply respected, empty payee, category+bucket assignment.

B1: BudgetService at app/Services/Budget/BudgetService.php — pure PHP, no Livewire coupling. Methods: obligationForMonth (latest BucketObligationVersion <= target month, falls back to bucket.monthly_obligation), availableForMonth (assignments_in - assignments_out + transaction amounts + split amounts through month-end), carryoverIntoMonth (delegate to prior-month availableForMonth), rolledForwardObligation (cumulative gap = obligation - assigned for each prior month, max(0, ...)), isUnderfunded (available < obligation + rolled), underfundedBuckets (non-archived non-system filter). 11 Pest tests cover all envelope-math cases per acceptance: obligation history, edited-versions-don't-touch-past, carryover, splits, negative bucket math, rolled-forward accumulation, isUnderfunded, archived sweep. Fixed Carbon createFromDate microsecond drift by comparing year*100+month integer keys instead of lessThan(). 97 tests pass; pint clean.

title: LW-P2: Payee suggestion surface in TransactionForm
description:
When payee text is entered, the create-transaction Livewire form pulls suggestions via PayeeRuleService and prefills (but does not lock) category + bucket. Use wire:model.live.blur on payee.

## Acceptance
- Typing a known payee prefills category + bucket on blur
- User can override before save
- Pest covers prefill + override path
acceptance_criteria: Payee prefill works through PayeeRuleService.

title: LW-R1: Reconciliation matched-only flow
description:
Reconciliation marks transactions in an account as reconciled up to a target cleared balance. Matched-only path only (PRD MVP): user must clear individual rows; system commits when sum matches statement balance. Discrepancy-accepted path is deferred.

## Acceptance
- /household/accounts/{account}/reconcile Livewire page
- ReconcileAccount action commits atomic batch
- Pest covers happy + sum-mismatch (no commit)
acceptance_criteria: Reconciliation matched-only works and tests pass.

title: LW-ST3: Settings/Appearance with Alpine.store('theme')
description:
Theme toggle is client-only state. Use Alpine.store('theme') with localStorage persistence. No Livewire round-trip on toggle.

## Acceptance
- Theme toggles instantly; persists across reloads
- Server never sees the value; no Livewire property
- Initial render reads localStorage before paint
acceptance_criteria: Theme is fully Alpine-side store.
close_reason: Already delivered by the Livewire/Fortify starter kit + LW-F4 invite-gating work. Verified by passing Pest tests in suite (57 tests, 148 assertions): AuthenticationTest, RegistrationTest (now invite-only), InviteOnlyRegistrationTest, PasswordResetTest, PasswordConfirmationTest, EmailVerificationTest, TwoFactorChallengeTest, ProfileUpdateTest, SecurityTest. resources/views/pages/auth/* are plain Blade forms posting to Fortify controllers (login.blade.php, register.blade.php with invite logic via EnsureValidInvite middleware, forgot-password.blade.php, reset-password.blade.php, verify-email.blade.php, two-factor-challenge.blade.php, confirm-password.blade.php). Passkey support shipped via x-passkey-verify component + PasskeyLoginResponse. resources/views/pages/settings/* are Livewire SFCs (⚡profile, ⚡security, ⚡appearance). Appearance page uses Flux radio-group bound to $flux.appearance store (Alpine-side theme). Any later refactor to convert plain Blade auth views into Livewire SFCs with custom Form objects can be filed as separate cleanup tickets; the user-facing behavior is already correct.

title: LW-A5: Passkeys (Livewire + WebAuthn)
description:
Mirror Earmark-web's @laravel/passkeys flow on the Livewire side. Profile page surfaces a passkeys panel; registration flow uses Alpine to drive the WebAuthn API and sends credential payload to a Livewire action.

## Acceptance
- User can register and remove a passkey
- Sign-in via passkey works
- Pest covers register + login via fake authenticator if available
acceptance_criteria: Passkeys flow works in Livewire.
